notes: 32-module fault-tolerant supermini for OLTP. Up to 32 processing modules per system; each module has 2 software-visible Motorola 68000 CPUs and up to 16MB memory.
Distributed OS that makes a federation of processing modules appear as a single virtual computer to programs and users; transparent file/process distribution; uniform service-request interface.
Multi-tasking transaction-server framework; START / COMMIT / ABORT primitives; per-application message queues that can be dynamically redirected; two-phase commit across modules.
High-speed coaxial inter-module link connecting the Stratus/32 processing modules; runs in dual-link parallel for fault tolerance.
Stratus inter-system networking software; lets multiple Stratus systems appear as one with transparent file/IPC and per-node password security.
Stratus screen/forms management subsystem for OLTP applications.
16/32-bit CPU; ~$100/unit; four 68000s per Stratus CPU board (two pairs, two software-visible); enabled cost-effective hardware redundancy.
Distributed transaction commit protocol; Stratus/32 implementation uses Phase I Commit flag in file header and disk-mirrored journaling.
Each Stratus disk has a duplicate on a separate controller; OS chooses less-busy or better-positioned disk for reads; transparent to applications.
Supported on Stratus/32 alongside PL/I, BASIC, FORTRAN, Pascal.
Supported on Stratus/32 TPF transaction servers.
